Sackler has been criticized for his company's role in the opioid epidemic, which has led to widespread addiction, overdose deaths, and other health problems. He has been accused of prioritizing profits over public health and of using deceptive marketing tactics to promote OxyContin.

In 2020, Richard Sackler and other members of the Sackler family agreed to pay $4.5 billion to settle lawsuits related to the opioid crisis. However, the settlement did not include any admission of wrongdoing by the Sackler family or Purdue Pharma.
